A demo that anyone can follow builds confidence.
It is a button that lets a customer reset a password.
A backlog is the list which holds the waiting tasks.
A stakeholder is a person who cares about the result.
We found a bug that slowed the login screen.
Thanks, Rafael. Before the demo, can you give me a quick summary, in plain English?
Of course. This sprint we built a new button that lets customers reset their own password.
Nice. And what is a backlog? Your team keeps using that word.
Good question. A backlog is the list of tasks that are still waiting to be built. Think of it as a to-do list for the whole product.
That helps. Is everything ready to launch?
Almost. We found a bug that slowed the login, so the photo feature needs one more week. Everything else works, and I will show it in the demo now.

Hi team, this is Rafael with a quick voice note before tomorrow's sprint review. Remember who is in the room: most of our guests are not engineers, so we explain everything in plain terms, no jargon. Here is the plan. I will open with a one-line overview: this sprint we made the login faster and we added a new button that lets customers reset their own password. Then I will use the house analogy, because people remember it. Our app is like a house, and this sprint we built the front door. After that, the honest part. We found a bug that slowed the login screen, so the photo feature is not ready yet. We do not hide it; we simply say the part that handles photos needs one more week. Please keep your section short and avoid words like backlog, endpoint, or refactor. If you must use a technical word, define it in one simple sentence. And finally, we end with the demo, the short live version of what already works, because people trust what they can see, not what they only hear. Thanks, everyone, and let's keep it clear and calm.

Hi Rafael, this is Sofia from marketing, leaving you a short voicemail after the sprint review. First of all, thank you. That was the clearest update I have seen from your team. The house analogy really helped, and I finally understand what the front door means for our customers. A couple of small things for next time. When you mentioned the backlog, I was a little lost for a second, so a one-line definition, like the list of tasks that are still waiting, would help people like me. I also loved the demo. Seeing the password button work live was worth more than ten slides, so please keep doing that. On the bug with the photo feature, I appreciate the honesty. You said one more week, which is fine, but it would help if you tell us what the customer will and will not be able to do until then. Marketing has to manage what we promise. Send me one plain-English line I can share with the wider team, and we are good. Great job today, Rafael, really.
